zh.lifeSummary: Agnes William Borthwick of Borthwick是William Borthwick 3rd Lord Borthwick和Margaret Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly Borthwick的女兒，出生。她於大約1484年與David Kennedy 3rd Lord Kennedy and 1st Earl of Cassilis在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安波爾維其可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1509年8月之前。

nameMeanings.name: Borthwick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the old barony of Borthwick, formerly Loquhariot (Midlothian) and possibly also from Borthwick (Roxburghshire). The placenames are from Old English bord ‘board, table’ + wīc ‘outlying dairy farm’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes William Borthwick of Borthwick was born as the daughter of William Borthwick 3rd Lord Borthwick and Margaret Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly Borthwick. She married David Kennedy 3rd Lord Kennedy and 1st Earl of Cassilis about 1484, in Borthwick, Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died before August 1509.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes William Borthwick of Borthwick est née, fille de William Borthwick 3rd Lord Borthwick et Margaret Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly Borthwick. Elle a épousé David Kennedy 3rd Lord Kennedy and 1st Earl of Cassilis environ 1484, à Borthwick, Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée avant août 1509.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes William Borthwick of BorthwickはWilliam Borthwick 3rd Lord BorthwickとMargaret Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly Borthwickの娘として生まれた。約1484年にイギリススコットランドミドロスアンボルスウィックでDavid Kennedy 3rd Lord Kennedy and 1st Earl of Cassilisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1509年8月以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes William Borthwick of Borthwick(은)는 William Borthwick 3rd Lord Borthwick(와)과 Margaret Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly Borthwick의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1484년 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언 볼스윅에서 David Kennedy 3rd Lord Kennedy and 1st Earl of Cassilis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1509년 8월 이전 사망했다.
